steps.title: Identify the subject type
steps.content: The subject is “Having proportionally large populations,” a participial phrase functioning as a noun in the subject position. When a participial phrase serves as the sentence subject, it takes a singular verb—similar to “Running is exhausting” or “Having resources is valuable.”

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: Is is the only singular present-tense verb that agrees with this singular subject. The other options (are, were, have been) are either plural verb forms or different tenses, violating subject-verb agreement. That confirms B is correct.
